originalTitle: 'The weapons were loud, but there was always music': Sudanese band play on through the war
title: Brave Music in Sudan! Singing Through the Noise
content:
Hello, super kids! Are you ready for a special story? Let's go on an adventure to a big, beautiful country in Africa called Sudan. It's far away, across the big blue ocean. In Sudan, there is a very special group of friends. They are a band, just like your favorite singers! Their name is Aswat Almadina. That means "Voices of the City." They love to sing and play happy music for everyone. Music can make you dance, smile, and feel joyful, right?

A Loud, Scary Day

Imagine you are playing with your friends, having lots of fun. That's a bit like Aswat Almadina. Three years ago, the band members were in their special music room. This room is where they practice their songs and make new tunes. Suddenly, they heard very loud and scary noises outside. BOOM! BANG! CRASH! It was not a thunderstorm or a fun fireworks show. These were scary sounds that made everyone feel worried. **Did you know?** Sudan is a very big country in Africa! It has a long, important river called the Nile, which brings water to many people.

What is War?

Those loud, scary sounds meant something very sad. It meant that a war had started. War is when grown-ups fight with big noises and sometimes break things. It makes people scared, and sometimes they have to leave their homes to be safe. It's a very sad time for everyone. Imagine your friends arguing very loudly, but much, much bigger and scarier. That's a bit like war.

Music in the Middle of Noise

When the war started, the band members, Aswat Almadina, felt scared too. Who wouldn't be? But they had a secret superpower! Can you guess what it was? Yes, it was their music! They thought, "Even if it's loud and scary outside, we can still make our music inside. Our music can help people feel a little bit better." **Did you know?** Music has been around for thousands and thousands of years! People from long, long ago used to make music with simple drums and their voices.

Finding Hope with Songs

So, the brave musicians decided to keep playing their instruments. They kept singing their songs. Their music was like a little light shining in the dark. It helped them feel strong and brave. It also helped people remember happy times, even when sad things were happening. Music has a magical way of helping our hearts feel warm and full of hope, even when we are scared.

Their Happy Songs

Aswat Almadina plays fun, lively music. They use drums that go "thump-thump-thump" and make you want to dance. They have guitars that go "strum-strum-strum" and sound so pretty. And their voices sing beautiful words! They sing about peace, about wanting everyone to be friends, and about hoping for sunshine and happiness to return to their country. Their songs are like a warm, gentle hug. **Did you know?** Different parts of Africa have many different types of amazing music, some with really cool drums and special dances!

Playing For Everyone

Even when it was hard to go outside because of the loud noises, the band found ways to keep playing. Maybe they played for their family in a safe room. Maybe they shared their songs with their neighbors who were feeling sad. Every song they played was like a special gift. A gift of happiness. A gift of hope. It showed everyone that even in tough times, we can find courage and share joy. Imagine singing your favorite song when you're feeling a bit down – it always helps, right?

Music Brings Us Together

When people heard Aswat Almadina's music, even for a little while, they felt a bit better. They might tap their feet, hum along, or even try to dance. Music can make everyone feel like they are part of one big, friendly team. It helps people feel less alone and reminds them that there are still good things in the world. It’s like music whispers, “You are not alone, and things will get better.” **Did you know?** Listening to happy music can make your brain feel good and help you think better during your schoolwork!

A Message of Bravery

Aswat Almadina showed everyone in Sudan something very important. They showed that even when things are very scary and loud, you can still find ways to be brave. You can still find ways to spread kindness, hope, and happiness. Their music was like a big, warm hug for their whole country. It was a reminder that even small acts of bravery, like playing a song, can make a big difference.

Keep the Music Playing!

So, remember this brave band from Sudan, Aswat Almadina. They taught us that music is a super powerful thing. It can help us feel happy, strong, and full of hope, even when the world is noisy and scary. Let's all keep listening to music, singing our favorite songs, and sharing kindness every day. Because kindness and music are two of the best superpowers in the world!
summary: A brave band from Sudan, Aswat Almadina, kept playing their happy music even when a war started, bringing hope and courage to people during scary times.
whyMatters: This story matters because it shows us that even when things are very hard and scary, like during a war, people can still find ways to be brave, spread happiness, and give hope to others through things like music.

title: Millions listen to Ethiopian star's song taking swipe at government
content: The country's biggest musician Teddy Afro laments a lack of unity in the country on his new track.

originalTitle: DR Congo accepts first set of deportees from the US
title: Welcome Home, Friends! News from DR Congo
content:
Hello from Africa!

Hi, everyone! I'm your friendly news reporter, here to share a special story from our amazing continent, Africa. Today, we're going to learn about something happening in a big country called the Democratic Republic of Congo, or DR Congo for short.

A Big Country Called DR Congo

DR Congo is a huge country right in the middle of Africa. It has beautiful rivers, tall trees, and many wonderful people. It's famous for its big forests and cool animals, like gorillas! **Did you know?** DR Congo is the second-largest country in Africa! That's bigger than many countries put together.

Friends Coming Home

Something new is happening there. Some people who used to live in DR Congo went to another country far away, called the United States of America. Now, they are coming back home. The government of DR Congo is welcoming them. It's like when your friend goes on a long trip and then comes back to your village or town. You're happy to see them again!

What Does "Coming Home" Mean?

These people had to leave the United States. This means the US government asked them to go back to their home country. It can happen if people don't have the right papers to stay in a country. It's a little bit like when you visit a friend's house, and it's time to go home. You can't stay there forever, right? **Did you know?** People move between countries for many reasons, like for work, school, or to visit family!

Far Away in America

The United States is a country very, very far from Africa. You would have to travel a long, long time in an airplane to get there. It's a big country with many cities and different kinds of weather. Imagine flying over big oceans and lands to get to a new place!

Why Do People Move?

Sometimes people move to other countries to find new jobs or to live a different kind of life. They might want to learn new things or help their families. It's a big adventure for them. But sometimes, things don't work out, and they need to come back.

A Place to Stay for Now

The government in DR Congo says that these friends coming back will be in the country for a little while. They are called "temporary" guests. This means they are not staying forever right now. "Temporary" means "just for a short time." Think of it like a quick visit. The government wants to help them settle in and figure out what's next. It's important to help people when they need it. **Did you know?** Africa has 54 countries! Each one is special and unique.

Helping Our Friends

When these people arrive in DR Congo, they might need help. They might feel a little bit sad or confused. They might not have a place to live right away. The government and kind people in DR Congo will help them. They will make sure they have food, a safe place to sleep, and help them find their families. It's like when your neighbour needs help after a storm. You offer them food or a place to stay, right? That's what being a good community is all about.

Being Kind and Sharing

This story reminds us that it's important to be kind to everyone. Whether they are from your village, a different country, or coming back home, everyone deserves kindness. We are all part of one big family on Earth. Helping each other makes our world a better place. **Did you know?** The DR Congo is home to many different languages, but French is the official language!

What Happens Next?

These friends are just starting their new journey back in DR Congo. It might take some time for them to feel completely at home again. But with the help of the government and the people, they will find their way. They are getting a chance to start fresh in their home country.

A Message for You

So, remember, my little friends! When you hear about people moving or coming back, think about how you can be kind. A smile, a helping hand, or just being friendly can make a big difference. Let's all be good neighbours, not just in our villages, but across our whole amazing continent of Africa. The people of DR Congo are showing us a great example of welcoming back their own. **Did you know?** The capital city of DR Congo is Kinshasa, which is a very big and busy city! This is a story about caring and helping. And that's a very important kind of news! Keep shining bright, little African stars!
summary: People who used to live in the US are returning to their home country, DR Congo, and the government is helping them settle back in for a temporary time.
whyMatters: This matters because it shows how countries help their citizens, and it reminds us to be kind and welcoming to people who are returning home or need help.
